mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy transmits into tighter financial conditions that weigh on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad demand-supply expansion that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns; neither series alone produces the same directional reliability.
failure_condition: Joint z-scores of RSAFS and INDPRO fail to produce a 20-day Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns only during monetary tightening cycles because supply-side resilience is the primary marginal driver when demand is suppressed by high rates.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing output and falling corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units indicates durable residential investment momentum that lifts construction and consumer durable demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: HOUST > 1200 is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices reduce real disposable income and squeeze profit margins when the Federal Reserve is constrained from easing, producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high CPIAUCSL and high FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A falling unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market that increases wage costs and reduces future corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 returns as margins compress.
failure_condition: UNRATE < 5.0% is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
